flashcards.front: What is the root class of the AWT component hierarchy?
flashcards.back: java.awt.Component is the root class of the AWT component hierarchy and serves as the foundation for all GUI components in Java.
JComponent extends java.awt.Container, which in turn extends java.awt.Component, maintaining the AWT component hierarchy.

flashcards.front: Name three Swing components that inherit from JComponent
flashcards.back: JButton, JLabel, and JPanel are all Swing components that inherit from javax.swing.JComponent.
Swing needs AWT because it builds on AWT's component hierarchy, event model, and native peer system for rendering components.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of Swing being built on AWT?
flashcards.back: The main advantage is that Swing can leverage AWT's platform-independent component model while adding lightweight, customizable components with pluggable look-and-feel.
